# Week One!

For week one I made a simple LED delay device using RC timing. It uses a combination of resistors and compacitors to store and slow down the voltage into the three LED's and the slight differences in the values of these components allow for different delays in time when the LED's turn on and off.

Here's a photo!

<img width="381" height="201" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2f2fe4b0-cc57-4e4b-9674-f6276a3d53fd" />

Here's a video of me making it!

https://lapse.hackclub.com/timelapse/HAgkg-XXbsR0

Here's a link to try it out!

[https://is.gd/FY7BEo](https://www.falstad.com/circuit/circuitjs.html?-qt6vKJZrWfhCPRSIa2ezUKbHpbEBoqMH2JJsFxiFQqIwWLwlcKfTkBgH1p65gsxfaFestUbCDcDdrI1Bo2DYwgNHhhJx2JRGMIGJJ+xzccLyzkqoXmslgIFwBB9EA)



# Week Two!

Now this is where things start to get interesting! For week two I made a morse code machine using a monostable multivibrator (oooo long words!) Practically it works by sending a different length of voltage to an LED depending on what button you press, one for dot and one for dits. 

Here's a photo!

<img width="784" height="725" alt="weekTwoSS" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d04dc063-6357-43a1-beb3-4819ed4019fc" />

Here's a video of me making it!

https://lapse.hackclub.com/timelapse/-EtHCt9JcfGj
